design_philosophy.core_loop: Infiltrate building from roof, collect secret documents from marked rooms via elevator/stairway navigation, eliminate enemy agents, escape to ground floor
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Time pressure combined with spatial puzzle-solving: player must navigate multi-floor building while avoiding/defeating enemies and managing time before alarm triggers increased difficulty
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Secret agent infiltration and exfiltration under pressure; tactical espionage using both weapons and environment
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Game enforces document collection completion—attempting escape without all documents returns player to highest uncollected location. Time pressure escalates with alarm mechanic. Emphasizes spatial mastery and multi-option problem-solving (shooting, jump-kicking, environmental hazards) over pure reflexes

difficulty.lives_system: Multiple lives; player loses one life per hit from enemy fire, crushing by elevator, or falling down open shaft
difficulty.checkpointing: Per-level progression; completion of all documents required to advance
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Moderate agency through route optimization and combat choices (shoot vs. melee vs. environmental kills). Time pressure is unavoidable but manageable through efficient navigation

progression.unlock_mechanism: Level-based advancement; player must clear each 30-story building stage sequentially. Game contains up to 10 levels. Progression blocked until all documents collected
replayability_hooks: Score-chasing via document collection efficiency and enemy elimination bonuses
Multiple combat approach options encourage varied playstyles across replays
Elevator timing mechanics reward route optimization on repeated attempts

world_building_techniques: Espionage premise implied through secret agent character (Agent 17, codename Otto) and document collection objective
Building setting itself creates implicit threat environment
Enemy agents establish implicit antagonistic faction
Getaway car escape reinforces infiltration/exfiltration narrative structure

faction_indicators: Player character: Secret agent Otto working for implicit intelligence agency
Enemy agents: Opposing faction protecting building secrets
Building occupants implied but not detailed
danger_signals: Armed enemy agents actively hunting player throughout building
Alarm system triggers escalated enemy aggression and slower elevator response
Environmental hazards: open elevator shafts, crushable by elevators, darkened sections obscuring threats
environmental_storytelling: 30-story building structure establishes vertical infiltration challenge
Red-marked doors indicate secure document locations without explicit labeling
Darkened floors suggest security or concealment measures
Elevator/escalator systems define navigation boundaries and tactical options
Basement escape route and waiting getaway car complete exfiltration scenario
